features.name: Per-User Isolation (ContextVar)
features.description: ContextVar-based HERMES_HOME override for per-user data isolation in Feishu gateway. Each user gets isolated memories, skills, sessions, and state.db.
features.evidence: All 12 files pass syntax check. Import tests pass. ContextVar isolation verified with concurrent asyncio tasks. Dynamic path resolution confirmed for skills_tool, skill_manager_tool, skills_hub, skills_sync, hermes_state, memory_tool.

features.name: Harness Infrastructure
features.description: CLAUDE.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, init.sh for structured multi-session development.
features.evidence: All harness files created and verified.

features.name: Per-User Isolation Tests
features.description: Add pytest tests for ContextVar isolation, concurrent asyncio tasks, memory/skills path resolution, and SessionDB isolation.
features.evidence: 23 unit tests (22 passed, 1 skipped due to missing httpx in system Python). Covers: ContextVar lifecycle, dynamic path resolution, async task isolation, thread propagation via copy_context(), SessionDB isolation, profile directory creation, CLI fallback behavior.

features.name: Integration Testing with Feishu Gateway
features.description: End-to-end test: two Feishu users send messages simultaneously, verify memories/skills/sessions are fully isolated.
features.evidence: 10 integration tests all passed. Covers: profile dir creation, ContextVar injection/cleanup, concurrent user isolation, memory invisibility between users, per-user SessionDB cache, session data isolation, background context propagation, shared infrastructure unaffected.

features.name: Fix: Operator Config Isolation (401 / Wrong Provider Bug)
features.description: Hotfix for per-user ContextVar being too broad: _get_model_config() was reading from user profile (no config.yaml) → empty config → provider=auto → OpenRouter → HTTP 401. Fixed by clearing ContextVar in _get_model_config() before calling load_config().
features.evidence: Syntax check passes. Rule documented in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md. Observed in production 2026-04-17 with real Feishu users. Pattern: operator-level config functions must clear ContextVar before reading.

features.name: Fix: get_config_path bypasses ContextVar + FEISHU_HOME_CHANNEL
features.description: Root fix for all auxiliary LLM / compression warnings: get_config_path() and get_env_path() now use _get_base_hermes_home() (env var, not ContextVar) so load_config() always returns the operator's base config regardless of per-user gateway context. Also sets FEISHU_HOME_CHANNEL in .env to suppress the home channel warning.
features.evidence: Verified: with ContextVar set to /tmp/user_abc, get_config_path() still returns base ~/.hermes/config.yaml. get_skills_dir() correctly returns /tmp/user_abc/skills. Fixes auxiliary LLM warning and home channel warning without touching auxiliary_client.py.

features.name: Fix: AIAgent Config + Document MEMORY/USER/SOUL files
features.description: Apply clear-ContextVar pattern to run_agent.py AIAgent init so operator memory/skill config is read from base home. Document MEMORY.md/USER.md/SOUL.md distinction: SOUL.md pre-created at profile init, MEMORY.md/USER.md auto-written by agent after N turns or /reset.
features.evidence: Syntax check passes. MEMORY.md not created = expected behavior (only casual food conversation, flush agent correctly decided nothing worth saving). Fixed architectural gap: run_agent.py now reads base config for memory settings.

features.name: Security Fix: Cross-User Data Access Prevention
features.description: Critical security fix for vulnerability where User A could access User B's private data (memories, skills, sessions) by specifying paths outside their profile. Added user boundary validation to all file tools (read_file, write_file, patch, search) and path disclosure prevention in display_hermes_home().
features.evidence: All tests pass: 10/10 user boundary tests, 3/3 path disclosure tests, 11/11 hermes_constants tests, 45/45 file operations tests. Syntax check passes. Attack vectors mitigated: direct path access, path traversal, symlink attacks, path enumeration, tool chaining. CLI mode backward compatible (no validation), gateway mode enforces strict validation.
